heading: Acquisition of space science data
label: 50113 Acquisition of space science data

children.text: the elemental and mineralogical resources of the moon, asteroids, planets and their moons, and comets;

children.heading: .—Acquisition From Commercial Providers
children.label: b .—Acquisition From Commercial Providers
children.text: The Administrator shall, to the extent possible and while satisfying the scientific or educational requirements of the Administration, and where appropriate, of other Federal agencies and scientific researchers, acquire, where cost effective, space science data from a commercial provider.

children.heading: .—Treatment of Space Science Data as Commercial Product or Commercial Service Under Acquisition Laws
children.label: c .—Treatment of Space Science Data as Commercial Product or Commercial Service Under Acquisition Laws
children.text: Acquisitions of space science data by the Administrator shall be carried out in accordance with applicable acquisition laws and regulations (including applicable provisions of chapters 201 through 285, 341 through 343, and 363 of title 10). For purposes of such law and regulations, space science data shall be considered to be a commercial product or commercial service. Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to preclude the United States from acquiring, through contracts with commercial providers, sufficient rights in data to meet the needs of the scientific and educational community or the needs of other government activities.

children.heading: .—Safety Standards
children.label: d .—Safety Standards
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to prohibit the Federal Government from requiring compliance with applicable safety standards.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: e .—Limitation
children.text: This section does not authorize the Administration to provide financial assistance for the development of commercial systems for the collection of space science data.
